spec_match.rationale: Custom element using await using to register/clean up observers + interval — concrete component lifecycle pattern enabled by the async proposal.

interactive.rationale: Add / remove the custom element from the DOM; observer + interval registration and cleanup logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises IntersectionObserver + ResizeObserver + interval auto-cleanup via await using; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: await using inside connected callback only disposes at FUNCTION EXIT, not when the element is disconnected. The demo's pattern may be misleading — typically you'd use disconnectedCallback to call dispose() on a stack. Worth checking the pattern's correctness.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/5071005145530368 — WRONG feature ID. This belongs to feature 5087324181102592. Critical rule #3 regression.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also points at chromestatus.com/feature/5071005145530368 but this concept belongs to feature 5087324181102592.

openQuestions.title: Verify the lifecycle pattern is sound
openQuestions.detail: await using inside connectedCallback only disposes when connectedCallback exits — but the function returns immediately. The disposers may never run. Confirm the demo actually cleans up on disconnect, perhaps by switching to a DisposableStack stored on the element and disposed in disconnectedCallback.

summary: Promising custom-element pattern but ships wrong chromestatus feature ID AND the await using pattern inside connectedCallback may dispose at the wrong time. Both worth confirming before merging.
